L1.area: Plugin infrastructure for real-time communication, user presence broadcasting, and live session monitoring

L2.description: A stored DOM cross-site scripting vulnerability exists where an unauthenticated attacker can inject arbitrary HTML and JavaScript via attacker-controlled metadata fields transmitted through a real-time messaging channel. The server-side handler persists user-supplied values into a connection tracking store without any HTML sanitization, and these values are subsequently broadcast to all connected clients. On the client side, the broadcast data is interpolated directly into an HTML template and appended to the DOM via a method that parses the string as live HTML nodes, allowing injected event handlers to execute in the authenticated context of administrators viewing the connection tracking interface.

L3.area: WebSocket connection handler and per-client metadata persistence in the real-time communication plugin
L3.description: A stored DOM cross-site scripting vulnerability exists where an unauthenticated attacker obtains a WebSocket connection token from an anonymous endpoint and connects with attacker-controlled URI and page title metadata in the query parameters. The server-side WebSocket connection handler persists these values into the connection store using only character encoding rather than HTML sanitization, and they are broadcast to all connected clients as part of the online-users payload. On the client side, the broadcast page title is interpolated unsanitized into an HTML template and appended to the DOM, allowing injected event handlers to execute in the authenticated context of administrators viewing the user presence panel.

ground_truth.reason: The WebSocket connection handler persists attacker-controlled page_title and selfURI values into the connection store using only utf8_encode (not HTML sanitization), and these values are broadcast to all clients where they are interpolated unsanitized into HTML and appended to the DOM, enabling stored DOM XSS in the admin's authenticated origin.
